mnemonics.title: Precedence Mnemonic
mnemonics.content: PUMAS - Parentheses, Unary, Multiplicative, Additive, Shift (for future). Remember: * / % before + -

mnemonics.title: Post vs Pre Increment
mnemonics.content: POST = Print Original Then Increment. PRE = Increment Then Print.

mnemonics.title: Integer Division
mnemonics.content: DIVIDE = Cuts off the DECIMAL part like a DIVORCE cuts off the relationship.

mnemonics.title: Modulo Sign Rule
mnemonics.content: The REMAINDER keeps the DIVIDEND's sign - Remember: DIVIDEND = DIVISOR × QUOTIENT + REMAINDER

acronyms.expansion: Parentheses, Exponents, Multiplication, Division, Addition, Subtraction (adapted for Java as PUMAS: Parentheses, Unary, Multiplicative, Additive)
acronyms.usage: Remembering operator precedence order in arithmetic expressions

acronyms.expansion: Brackets, Indices, Division, Multiplication, Addition, Subtraction
acronyms.usage: Alternative mnemonic for precedence used in mathematics

acronyms.expansion: Brackets, Order, Division, Multiplication, Addition, Subtraction

visualTips.visualization: Draw a timeline: [value used] → [increment happens] for post. [increment happens] → [value used] for pre. Write current value above timeline, next value below.
Imagine a number line wrapped in a circle. When you exceed MAX_VALUE, you wrap around to MIN_VALUE. Like a 12-hour clock: 13:00 becomes 1:00.
Draw a staircase: byte → short → int → long → float → double. When two types meet, both climb up to the higher step before operation.
Think of modulo as 'remainder after division'. On a number line wrapped around the divisor, it tells you where you land.

analogies.analogy: Like splitting 7 cookies among 2 children fairly - each gets 3 whole cookies, 1 cookie remains (but is discarded in integer division).
Like a 7-hour clock: hour 10 on a 7-hour clock goes 10-7=3. The modulo tells you what hour it 'really' is in a cyclic system.
Think of a+=5 as saying 'add 5 to my account balance' - your new balance is old balance plus 5.
Like pressing +1 on a counter BEFORE reading it: the display changes, then you read. Post-increment is reading, then pressing +1.
